task_completion.visual_evidence: FreeMED dashboard or interface is visible, indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent opened the Patient Recall creation form for Thomas Vance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A form or dialog for creating a 'Patient Recall' is visible, and the patient name 'Thomas Vance' is selected or displayed in the context.

task_completion.description: Agent entered 'Colonoscopy' and a date 3 years in the future.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The procedure field contains 'Colonoscopy' and the date field shows a year exactly 3 years greater than the current year.

task_completion.description: Agent entered the required clinical notes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The notes or comments field contains text mentioning 'polyp' and 'follow-up' (e.g., 'Benign polyp found, 3-year follow-up required').

task_completion.description: Agent successfully saved the patient recall.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the recall was saved, or the newly created recall is visible in Thomas Vance's record.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the FreeMED web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the FreeMED application, not terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent entered data into the actual FreeMED forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is typed into the correct input fields within the FreeMED web interface, not a text editor or separate document.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to alter the page.
integrity.visual_evidence: No browser developer tools (Inspect Element, Console) are open in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The patient's name is Thomas Vance. The procedure must be 'Colonoscopy'. The recall date must be exactly 3 years in the future from the current year. The notes must include the keywords 'polyp' and 'follow-up'.
